title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon honoring her mother who died of brain cancer.
Her mother lived 3.5 years after diagnosis due to care from Massachusetts General Hospital's cancer center.
Oakes, now an oncology nurse, participates for the second year with the Massachusetts General Hospital marathon team.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man with a sword on Hemenway Street near Northeastern University.
The man injured a police officer and EMS clinician before being shot; both had non-life-threatening injuries.
A mental health crisis was involved; Boston Police and Suffolk DA are investigating the incident.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother, Diana.
Dubois aims to raise funds for the Cancer Care Equity program at Massachusetts General Hospital, where Diana received treatment.
She qualified for the Boston Marathon after participating in the Lake Erie Marathon in September 2025.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fire at 80 Glendale Ave. in Brockton resulted in one death and two hospitalizations early Saturday morning.
Firefighters found the second floor of the house fully engulfed, with flames venting through the windows.
Authorities are investigating the cause of the fire, which started in a second-floor bedroom and appears non-suspicious.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with New Hampshire police on Saturday night.
Masse allegedly shot a Nottingham Police officer earlier that day, who sustained non-life-threatening injuries.
Police asked residents of Ham Road and Nottingham Road to shelter in place during the search for Masse.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, a 47-year-old from Canton, was diagnosed with breast cancer in 2024 but will run the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Gannon's best friend, Peggy, also diagnosed with breast cancer, will join her in running for Mass General Hospital.
Both are running to celebrate life and survivorship, underscoring triumph over cancer with perseverance and friendship.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' on April 4, marking his fifth time on the show.
Musical guest Jack White joined Black, performing for the fifth time solo on 'SNL'.
Black kicked off the night with a musical sketch featuring 'Seven Nation Army'.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown addressed past criticisms about his left-hand struggles, attributing them to a serious wrist injury.
Brown had major surgery on his left wrist in 2021, with reinjury in 2023, impacting his dribbling ability.
Brown improved his left-hand performance post-2023 surgery, achieving career highs in points, rebounds, and assists.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo, former NESN Red Sox announcer, returns to Boston with the San Diego Padres after 11 years away.
Orsillo, voice of the Red Sox from 2001 to 2015, is remembered fondly by fans and former colleagues alike.
Since 2016, Orsillo has worked as a game announcer for the Padres, which he describes as a wonderful Plan B.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora moved Roman Anthony to designated hitter to address defensive shortcomings.
Despite fielding challenges, Anthony contributed offensively with a triple and a base hit against the Padres.
Anthony, a former top prospect, is batting .231 as the Red Sox hold a 2-6 record early in the season.
